perturbation.original_text: OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it will not, at any time, without the prior written consent of the other party, use or disclose the other party’s Proprietary Information for any reason or in any manner whatsoever except as may be necessary for the discussion and exploration of the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it will not, for a period of one year, without the prior written consent of the other party, use or disclose the other party’s Proprietary Information for any reason or in any manner whatsoever except as may be necessary for the discussion and exploration of the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits the use or disclosure of Proprietary Information 'at any time.' Changing this to 'for a period of one year' introduces a contradiction with the perpetual protection originally intended. This means after one year the information can be disclosed, conflicting with the overall aim of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Preservation of Confidentiality . OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it shall regard, maintain and preserve the secrecy and confidentiality of any and all information and data, whether in oral or written form, including but not limited to, clinical study synopses, clinical study plans, costs, products, processes, methods, concepts, ideas, programs, formulae, apparati, chemicals, organisms, molecules, prototypes, techniques, know-how, marketing plans, business plans, data, strategies, forecasts, customer or supplier lists or technical requirements of customers, or other trade secrets (collectively referred to herein as the “Proprietary Information”) of the other party which may be disclosed to or obtained by it pursuant to this Agreement. Each party hereto shall take reasonable and necessary measures to preserve the secrecy and confidentiality and avoid the unauthorized use or disclosure of the other party’s Proprietary Information, including, without limitation, taking such measures of protection as it takes to protect its own confidential, proprietary or trade secret information. Each party hereto shall limit access to the other party’s Proprietary Information to those of its employees, agents, advisors, and consultants, who have a reasonable need for access to such information in connection with the discussion and exploration of the Purpose and who shall be subject to the non-disclosure covenants contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that proprietary information should not be disclosed 'at any time,' implying perpetual confidentiality. The changed text limits this restriction to 'for a period of one year.' This directly contradicts the 'Preservation of Confidentiality' section, which states that the parties 'shall regard, maintain and preserve the secrecy and confidentiality' of proprietary information, implying no time limit. The one-year limit in the changed text directly conflicts with the perpetual confidentiality intended in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Each party hereto shall limit access to the other party’s Proprietary Information to those of its employees, agents, advisors, and consultants, who have a reasonable need for access to such information in connection with the discussion and exploration of the Purpose and who shall be subject to the non-disclosure covenants contained herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party hereto shall allow access to the other party’s Proprietary Information to any of its employees, agents, advisors, and consultants, regardless of need for access to such information in connection with the discussion and exploration of the Purpose and who shall not be subject to the non-disclosure covenants contained herein.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, access to proprietary information was limited to those with a 'reasonable need' and subject to non-disclosure agreements. By changing this to 'allow access to any' and removing the non-disclosure requirement, there is a direct conflict with the stated goal of preserving confidentiality outlined in section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Preservation of Confidentiality . OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it shall regard, maintain and preserve the secrecy and confidentiality of any and all information and data, whether in oral or written form, including but not limited to, clinical study synopses, clinical study plans, costs, products, processes, methods, concepts, ideas, programs, formulae, apparati, chemicals, organisms, molecules, prototypes, techniques, know-how, marketing plans, business plans, data, strategies, forecasts, customer or supplier lists or technical requirements of customers, or other trade secrets (collectively referred to herein as the “Proprietary Information”) of the other party which may be disclosed to or obtained by it pursuant to this Agreement. Each party hereto shall take reasonable and necessary measures to preserve the secrecy and confidentiality and avoid the unauthorized use or disclosure of the other party’s Proprietary Information, including, without limitation, taking such measures of protection as it takes to protect its own confidential, proprietary or trade secret information. Each party hereto shall limit access to the other party’s Proprietary Information to those of its employees, agents, advisors, and consultants, who have a reasonable need for access to such information in connection with the discussion and exploration of the Purpose and who shall be subject to the non-disclosure covenants contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text both state that access to proprietary information is limited to those with a 'reasonable need' and are subject to non-disclosure agreements. The changed text removes both of these restrictions, allowing access to 'any' employee and removing the non-disclosure requirement. This creates a clear conflict with the confidentiality obligations outlined elsewhere in the contract, specifically the 'Preservation of Confidentiality' section, which requires maintaining the secrecy of proprietary information.

perturbation.original_text: OCERA and COMPANY each acknowledges that all property rights in the other party’s Proprietary Information are owned by the other party, and that none of such rights are owned by the party to which such Proprietary Information is disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: OCERA and COMPANY each acknowledges that all property rights in the other party’s Proprietary Information are owned equally by both parties, and that each of such rights are owned by the party to which such Proprietary Information is disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that all property rights remain with the disclosing party. This change introduces a direct contradiction by claiming that property rights are now owned equally and by the receiving party, impacting ownership and control over the disclosed information. The contradiction is in direct conflict with section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Proprietary Rights . OCERA and COMPANY each acknowledges that all property rights in the other party’s Proprietary Information are owned by the other party, and that none of such rights are owned by the party to which such Proprietary Information is disclosed. Each of the parties hereto further acknowledges that the other party has not granted and does not grant in this Agreement any license to the other party under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other proprietary right to use or reproduce any Proprietary Information of such party other than for the conduct of the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, which is even the original text itself, concerning the ownership of proprietary information. The original and contradicted text state that each party owns the rights to their own proprietary information. The changed text states that both parties own equal rights, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency with the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended only by a written instrument executed by authorized representatives of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended by either party providing verbal consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text insists on a written amendment executed by both parties. The change allows for verbal amendments. This is contradicting with section 12 that requires written consent for amendments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement . This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties concerning the matters discussed herein and supersedes and replaces all prior agreements, understandings, writings and discussions between the parties, with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. This Agreement may be amended only by a written instrument executed by authorized representatives of the parties. This Agreement shall not create any obligation for either party to enter into any agreement or relationship with the other. Either party may end discussions of a possible relationship at any time and for any or no reason and each party reserves the right to disclose its own confidential information to any third party at any time. This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterpart copies, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which shall together be deemed to constitute one Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text both state that amendments must be in writing. The changed text explicitly allows for verbal amendments, which directly contradicts the written requirement in both the original and contradicted texts. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making enforcement unclear as it's uncertain whether a verbal or written amendment is valid.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, OCERA and COMPANY may each retain one (1) copy of such information in their confidential files solely for record keeping purposes to ensure compliance with this Agreement, with such copy remaining subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, OCERA and COMPANY may each distribute copies of such information to all employees for record keeping purposes to ensure compliance with this Agreement, with such copies not being subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows one confidential copy for record keeping, still under confidentiality. The modification allows distribution to all employees without confidentiality. This contradicts the confidentiality obligations laid out in sections 1, 2, and 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Preservation of Confidentiality . OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it shall regard, maintain and preserve the secrecy and confidentiality of any and all information and data, whether in oral or written form, including but not limited to, clinical study synopses, clinical study plans, costs, products, processes, methods, concepts, ideas, programs, formulae, apparati, chemicals, organisms, molecules, prototypes, techniques, know-how, marketing plans, business plans, data, strategies, forecasts, customer or supplier lists or technical requirements of customers, or other trade secrets (collectively referred to herein as the “Proprietary Information”) of the other party which may be disclosed to or obtained by it pursuant to this Agreement. Each party hereto shall take reasonable and necessary measures to preserve the secrecy and confidentiality and avoid the unauthorized use or disclosure of the other party’s Proprietary Information, including, without limitation, taking such measures of protection as it takes to protect its own confidential, proprietary or trade secret information. Each party hereto shall limit access to the other party’s Proprietary Information to those of its employees, agents, advisors, and consultants, who have a reasonable need for access to such information in connection with the discussion and exploration of the Purpose and who shall be subject to the non-disclosure covenants contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause regarding the confidentiality of information. Originally, OCERA and COMPANY could retain one confidential copy. The changed text allows distribution of copies to all employees, and these copies are explicitly stated as *not* being subject to confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the 'Preservation of Confidentiality' section, which mandates that OCERA and COMPANY preserve the secrecy and confidentiality of Proprietary Information and limit access to employees with a need to know and who are subject to non-disclosure covenants. Distributing copies to all employees without confidentiality breaks this obligation, resulting in a clear in-text contradiction.
